# full-stack-fastapi — Workspace Bootstrap

**Auto-generated by workspace.py. Give this to an agent to set up from scratch.**

---

## Quick Start — Copy & Paste This

```
Set up full-stack-fastapi workspace from scratch. Clone repos: full-stack-fastapi-template, aila-catalog-schema. Create venv, install editable packages: aila-catalog-schema. Install deps: pandas, pyarrow, jira, pyyaml, gitpython. Install LSP: typescript-language-server, pyright. Run workspace.py generate, then WORKSPACE_SELFTEST.md to verify. Fix any failing tests before proceeding.
```

---

## Repositories

- **full-stack-fastapi-template**: `https://github.com/fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template.git`
  This workspace
- **aila-catalog-schema**: `git@gitlab.dataart.com:da/dalf/aila-catalog-schema.git` — `pip install -e .`
  Workspace types (KiroWorkspace, KiroAgent)

---

## Setup Steps

### 1. Clone all repos
### 2. Create venv: `python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate`
### 3. Install SDKs: pip install -e /path/to/aila-catalog-schema
### 4. Install deps: `pip install pandas pyarrow jira python-dotenv pyyaml gitpython`
### 5. LSP: `npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript && pip install pyright`
### 6. Generate: `python3 workspace.py generate`
### 7. Self-Test (CRITICAL)

Start Kiro and run every test in `WORKSPACE_SELFTEST.md`:

```bash
kiro
```

**For each failing test:**
1. Identify the root cause (missing repo, wrong path, missing package)
2. Fix the issue
3. Re-run `python3 workspace.py generate`
4. Re-test the failing check

**Do not stop until all self-test checks pass.** This is how configuration
issues are caught and resolved. A passing self-test means the workspace is
fully operational.

---

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| git clone auth fails | Authenticate via browser, retry |
| pip install fails | Activate venv first |
| workspace.py fails | Check `which python3` points to venv |
| Jira MCP fails | Verify token and SSL settings |
| Code agents empty | Check external-links/ symlinks |
| LSP fails | `/code init -f` |
